title: paspalum riparium
intro: Paspalum riparium, also known as river paspalum, is a type of tropical grass native to Central and South America, characterized by its ability to thrive in wet environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Paspalum riparium include its potential to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and combat bacterial infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal benefits.
